questions.question: Which telescope has recorded an image of two merging galaxies nicknamed ‘Angel Wing’?
questions.explanation: The Hubble Space Telescope has recorded an image of two merging galaxies in the VV-689 system that appears to be shaped like wings. The interaction between the two galaxies is symmetrical, and the image appears to resemble wings. The two galaxies are currently in the middle of a collision and are nicknamed Angel Wing. Other galactical alignments only appear to overlap because of the angle they are viewed from on the earth.

questions.question: As per India’s National framework for malaria elimination, India will eliminate Malaria by which year?
questions.explanation: The World Health Organisation (WHO) representative to India said that in line with WHO Global Malaria Technical Strategy, India has developed a national framework for malaria elimination. It aims to achieve zero cases by 2027 and it will eliminate malaria by 2030. The RTS, S (Mosquirix) is the world’s first WHO-approved vaccine against Malaria caused by the Plasmodium falciparum parasite. World Malaria Day is observed on April 25.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry implements the ‘Unnat Bharat Abhiyan 2.0’?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

questions.explanation: In 2018, the Ministry of Human Resources and Development (Ministry of Education) launched the Unnat Bharat Abhiyan 2.0’. It has completed four years. It was launched with the vision to bring transformational change in rural development processes. UBA 2.0 is the Challenge Mode of Unnat Bharat Abhiyan programme where all Higher Education Institutions are required to willingly adopt at least 5 villages.

questions.question: What is the theme of the ‘World Intellectual Property Day’ 2022?

options.label: A
options.text: IP and Youth innovating for a better future

answer.label: A
answer.text: IP and Youth innovating for a better future

questions.explanation: The ‘World Intellectual Property Day’ 2022 is celebrated on April 26, to raise awareness of the impact of how patents, copyright, trademarks and design affect our daily life. Geneva-based World Intellectual Property Organization is one of the 15 specialized agencies of the United Nations. The theme for this year’s World Intellectual Property Day is focused on ‘IP and Youth innovating for a better future’.

questions.question: Which country organises ‘Han Kuang’ (Han Glory) war-games?
questions.explanation: Taiwan Military Forces recently announced a date for its ‘Han Kuang’ (Han Glory) wargames, and the commanders also insisted that the conflict in Ukraine would factor into the exercises. The War-games simulate defending their island from Chinese attacks. Over 23 million people of Taiwan live under constant threat of an invasion by China. China had announced that the self-ruled democratic island as part of its territory to be re-taken one day, by force if necessary.

questions.question: ‘World Immunization Week’ is observed every year in which month?
questions.explanation: The World Health Organization (WHO) organises the World Immunization Week between 24-30 April, every year. As per the WHO, Increasing awareness about vaccines and closing immunization gaps could protect millions of people worldwide from vaccine-preventable diseases, including polio. After global efforts, there were just six cases of polio caused by the wild poliovirus in 2021.

questions.question: India has recently opened its new mission in which European country?
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet approved a proposal to open a new Indian mission in Lithuania in 2022, to deepen political relations and strategic cooperation and enable growth of bilateral trade with the Baltic country. The decision to open an embassy in Lithuania is a part of the government’s pursuit of its national priority of growth and development or ‘Sabka Saath Sabka Vikas’ initiative. India’s diplomatic presence in Lithuania will provide market access for companies and improve exports of goods and services.

questions.question: ‘Watering hole’, which was seen sometimes in the news, is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Watering hole attack, also known as a supply chain attack, is one of the forms of Cyber Attacks. The watering hole is a technique in which the attacker observes the websites an organisation often uses and infects them with malware. Top websites such as Facebook, Microsoft, Apple, and Twitter were exploited to execute watering hole attacks recently.

questions.question: How many permanent members are part of the UN Security Council and thus have Veto Power?
questions.explanation: The five permanent members of the U.N. Security Council namely the U.S., the U.K., France, China and Russia, have veto power. The UNGA recently passed the ‘Resolution on standing mandate for GA meeting in case of use of Veto’. Whenever the permanent member uses the veto power to block a resolution, it will require a meeting of the General Assembly. Russia has used its veto power to hinder UNSC resolutions against its military tactics in Ukraine and in Syria.

questions.question: ‘Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) Certification Scheme’ is associated with which Union Ministry?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises has launched a revamped Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) Certification Scheme. The first phase of the programme will focus on manufacturing MSMEs while the second would be for those MSMEs in the services sector. The number of parameters required for a Gold category ZED certificate has been recently reduced from 50 to 20.
